state.output:
gws — Google Workspace CLI

USAGE:
    gws <service> <resource> [sub-resource] <method> [flags]
    gws schema <service.resource.method> [--resolve-refs]

EXAMPLES:
    gws drive files list --params '{"pageSize": 10}'
    gws drive files get --params '{"fileId": "abc123"}'
    gws sheets spreadsheets get --params '{"spreadsheetId": "..."}'
    gws gmail users messages list --params '{"userId": "me"}'
    gws schema drive.files.list

FLAGS:
    --params <JSON>       URL/Query parameters as JSON
    --json <JSON>         Request body as JSON (POST/PATCH/PUT)
    --upload <PATH>       Local file to upload as media content (multipart)
    --upload-content-type <MIME>  MIME type of the uploaded file (auto-detected from extension if omitted)
    --output <PATH>       Output file path for binary responses
    --format <FMT>        Output format: json (default), table, yaml, csv
    --api-version <VER>   Override the API version (e.g., v2, v3)
    --page-all            Auto-paginate, one JSON line per page (NDJSON)
    --page-limit <N>      Max pages to fetch with --page-all (default: 10)
    --page-delay <MS>     Delay between pages in ms (default: 100)

SERVICES:
    drive                Manage files, folders, and shared drives
    sheets               Read and write spreadsheets
    gmail                Send, read, and manage email
    calendar             Manage calendars and events
    admin-reports        Audit logs and usage reports (also: reports)
    docs                 Read and write Google Docs
    slides               Read and write presentations
    tasks                Manage task lists and tasks
    people               Manage contacts and profiles
    chat                 Manage Chat spaces and messages
    classroom            Manage classes, rosters, and coursework
    forms                Read and write Google Forms
    keep                 Manage Google Keep notes
    meet                 Manage Google Meet conferences
    events               Subscribe to Google Workspace events
    modelarmor           Filter user-generated content for safety
    workflow             Cross-service productivity workflows (also: wf)

ENVIRONMENT:
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_TOKEN               Pre-obtained OAuth2 access token (highest priority)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CREDENTIALS_FILE    Path to OAuth credentials JSON file
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CLIENT_ID           OAuth client ID (for gws auth login)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CLIENT_SECRET       OAuth client secret (for gws auth login)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CONFIG_DIR          Override config directory (default: ~/.config/gws)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_KEYRING_BACKEND     Keyring backend: keyring (default) or file
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_SANITIZE_TEMPLATE   Default Model Armor template
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_SANITIZE_MODE       Sanitization mode: warn (default) or block
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_PROJECT_ID              Override the GCP project ID for quota and billing
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_LOG                 Log level for stderr (e.g., gws=debug)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_LOG_FILE            Directory for JSON log files (daily rotation)

EXIT CODES:
    0    Success
    1    API error  — Google returned an error response
    2    Auth error — credentials missing or invalid
    3    Validation — bad arguments or input
    4    Discovery  — could not fetch API schema
    5    Internal   — unexpected failure

COMMUNITY:
    Star the repo: https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli
    Report bugs / request features: https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli/issues
    Please search existing issues first; if one already exists, comment there.

DISCLAIMER:
    This is not an officially supported Google product.

metadata.output:
gws — Google Workspace CLI

USAGE:
    gws <service> <resource> [sub-resource] <method> [flags]
    gws schema <service.resource.method> [--resolve-refs]

EXAMPLES:
    gws drive files list --params '{"pageSize": 10}'
    gws drive files get --params '{"fileId": "abc123"}'
    gws sheets spreadsheets get --params '{"spreadsheetId": "..."}'
    gws gmail users messages list --params '{"userId": "me"}'
    gws schema drive.files.list

FLAGS:
    --params <JSON>       URL/Query parameters as JSON
    --json <JSON>         Request body as JSON (POST/PATCH/PUT)
    --upload <PATH>       Local file to upload as media content (multipart)
    --upload-content-type <MIME>  MIME type of the uploaded file (auto-detected from extension if omitted)
    --output <PATH>       Output file path for binary responses
    --format <FMT>        Output format: json (default), table, yaml, csv
    --api-version <VER>   Override the API version (e.g., v2, v3)
    --page-all            Auto-paginate, one JSON line per page (NDJSON)
    --page-limit <N>      Max pages to fetch with --page-all (default: 10)
    --page-delay <MS>     Delay between pages in ms (default: 100)

SERVICES:
    drive                Manage files, folders, and shared drives
    sheets               Read and write spreadsheets
    gmail                Send, read, and manage email
    calendar             Manage calendars and events
    admin-reports        Audit logs and usage reports (also: reports)
    docs                 Read and write Google Docs
    slides               Read and write presentations
    tasks                Manage task lists and tasks
    people               Manage contacts and profiles
    chat                 Manage Chat spaces and messages
    classroom            Manage classes, rosters, and coursework
    forms                Read and write Google Forms
    keep                 Manage Google Keep notes
    meet                 Manage Google Meet conferences
    events               Subscribe to Google Workspace events
    modelarmor           Filter user-generated content for safety
    workflow             Cross-service productivity workflows (also: wf)

ENVIRONMENT:
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_TOKEN               Pre-obtained OAuth2 access token (highest priority)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CREDENTIALS_FILE    Path to OAuth credentials JSON file
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CLIENT_ID           OAuth client ID (for gws auth login)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CLIENT_SECRET       OAuth client secret (for gws auth login)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_CONFIG_DIR          Override config directory (default: ~/.config/gws)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_KEYRING_BACKEND     Keyring backend: keyring (default) or file
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_SANITIZE_TEMPLATE   Default Model Armor template
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_SANITIZE_MODE       Sanitization mode: warn (default) or block
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_PROJECT_ID              Override the GCP project ID for quota and billing
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_LOG                 Log level for stderr (e.g., gws=debug)
    GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_CLI_LOG_FILE            Directory for JSON log files (daily rotation)

EXIT CODES:
    0    Success
    1    API error  — Google returned an error response
    2    Auth error — credentials missing or invalid
    3    Validation — bad arguments or input
    4    Discovery  — could not fetch API schema
    5    Internal   — unexpected failure

COMMUNITY:
    Star the repo: https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli
    Report bugs / request features: https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli/issues
    Please search existing issues first; if one already exists, comment there.

DISCLAIMER:
    This is not an officially supported Google product.

state.title: Shows Gmail message get schema
Shows Gmail messages list schema
Shows Gmail thread get schema

input.command:
uv run python - <<'PY'
import json, subprocess, sys, re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
from email.utils import getaddresses
from datetime import datetime, timezone


def run_gws(args):
    cmd = ["gws"] + args
    p = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if p.returncode != 0:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Command failed: {' '.join(cmd)}\n{p.stderr}\n{p.stdout}")
    out = p.stdout.strip()
    if not out:
        return None
    return out

# Get authenticated user's email
profile_out = run_gws(["gmail", "users", "getProfile", "--params", '{"userId":"me"}'])
profile = json.loads(profile_out)
my_email = profile.get("emailAddress", "").lower()
if not my_email:
    raise RuntimeError("Could not determine authenticated email address")

# List sent messages in last 30 days
params = json.dumps({"userId":"me", "q":"in:sent newer_than:30d", "maxResults":500})
list_out = run_gws(["gmail", "users", "messages", "list", "--params", params, "--page-all", "--page-limit", "50"])

message_ids = []
if list_out:
    for line in list_out.splitlines():
        line=line.strip()
        if not line:
            continue
        page = json.loads(line)
        for m in page.get("messages", []) or []:
            mid = m.get("id")
            if mid:
                message_ids.append(mid)

sent_count = len(message_ids)

# Early return structure if no sent emails
if sent_count == 0:
    result = {
        "TotalSent": 0,
        "TopRecipients": [],
        "ReplyRate": 0.0,
        "AvgResponseTimeHours": 0.0,
    }
    print(json.dumps(result))
    sys.exit(0)

# Fetch metadata for each sent message
headers_needed = ["From", "To", "Cc", "Bcc", "Date"]
sent_msgs = []
thread_ids = set()
for mid in message_ids:
    params = {
        "userId": "me",
        "id": mid,
        "format": "metadata",
        "metadataHeaders": headers_needed,
    }
    out = run_gws(["gmail", "users", "messages", "get", "--params", json.dumps(params)])
    m = json.loads(out)
    sent_msgs.append(m)
    tid = m.get("threadId")
    if tid:
        thread_ids.add(tid)

# Helper to read headers

def header_map(msg):
    hs = (((msg.get("payload") or {}).get("headers")) or [])
    d = {}
    for h in hs:
        name = (h.get("name") or "").lower()
        if name and name not in d:
            d[name] = h.get("value") or ""
    return d

# Top recipients
recip_counter = Counter()
for m in sent_msgs:
    hm = header_map(m)
    addrs = []
    for field in ("to", "cc", "bcc"):
        v = hm.get(field, "")
        if v:
            addrs.extend(getaddresses([v]))
    seen = set()
    for _, addr in addrs:
        a = addr.strip().lower()
        if not a or a == my_email:
            continue
        if a in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(a)
        recip_counter[a] += 1

top3 = recip_counter.most_common(3)

# Fetch all relevant threads once
thread_data = {}
for tid in thread_ids:
    params = {
        "userId": "me",
        "id": tid,
        "format": "metadata",
        "metadataHeaders": ["From", "To", "Cc", "Bcc", "Date", "Subject", "Message-ID", "In-Reply-To", "References"],
    }
    out = run_gws(["gmail", "users", "threads", "get", "--params", json.dumps(params)])
    thread_data[tid] = json.loads(out)

# Determine if a sent email got at least one reply into inbox
sent_with_reply = 0

# Compute response times (my reply after receiving an inbound email)
response_hours = []

for sm in sent_msgs:
    sid = sm.get("id")
    stid = sm.get("threadId")
    try:
        sent_ts = int(sm.get("internalDate", "0"))
    except Exception:
        sent_ts = 0
    if not stid or stid not in thread_data or not sent_ts:
        continue

    msgs = thread_data[stid].get("messages", []) or []

    # Build parsed timeline entries
    entries = []
    for tm in msgs:
        t_hm = header_map(tm)
        from_field = t_hm.get("from", "")
        from_addr = ""
        parsed = getaddresses([from_field])
        if parsed:
            from_addr = (parsed[0][1] or "").strip().lower()
        labels = set((tm.get("labelIds") or []))
        try:
            ts = int(tm.get("internalDate", "0"))
        except Exception:
            ts = 0
        entries.append({
            "id": tm.get("id"),
            "ts": ts,
            "from": from_addr,
            "is_me": from_addr == my_email,
            "in_inbox": "INBOX" in labels,
            "labels": labels,
        })

    entries.sort(key=lambda x: (x["ts"], x["id"] or ""))

    # reply back into inbox after this sent message
    has_reply = any((e["ts"] > sent_ts and (not e["is_me"]) and e["in_inbox"]) for e in entries)
    if has_reply:
        sent_with_reply += 1

    # response time: find latest inbound before this sent message
    prior_inbound = [e for e in entries if e["ts"] < sent_ts and (not e["is_me"]) and e["in_inbox"]]
    if prior_inbound:
        latest = max(prior_inbound, key=lambda x: x["ts"])
        delta_h = (sent_ts - latest["ts"]) / (1000 * 60 * 60)
        if delta_h >= 0:
            response_hours.append(delta_h)

reply_rate = (sent_with_reply / sent_count * 100.0) if sent_count else 0.0
avg_response = (sum(response_hours) / len(response_hours)) if response_hours else 0.0

result = {
    "TotalSent": sent_count,
    "TopRecipients": top3,
    "ReplyRate": reply_rate,
    "AvgResponseTimeHours": avg_response,
}
print(json.dumps(result))
PY
input.description: Analyze Gmail sent email communication patterns
